rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign only for existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the analyzed model's dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Use only scalar types int, real, and bool in AGREE const and eq declarations to avoid resolution errors.
Align helper declaration types with the types of the expressions they represent to prevent type mismatch errors.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level assignments should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
AGREE guarantees must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
Avoid referencing undefined or unqualified identifiers in AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; only repair existing model elements.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for bindings instead.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers unless they are already present in the model.
